operating_state: NIKE Inc revenues increased 3% to $10.4 billion in Q3 2021, driven by 42% growth in Greater China offsetting declines across other regions. NIKE Direct revenues grew 20% to represent 40% of total revenues, with digital sales surging 54% as strong growth in APLA offset comparable store declines from reduced physical retail traffic. Supply chain disruptions from container shortages and port congestion delayed wholesale shipments, particularly impacting North America wholesale. Converse revenues increased 8% currency-neutral with growth across all geographies driven by strong digital sales.
cost_structure: Gross margin expanded 130 basis points to 45.6% driven by higher full-price product margins and favorable NIKE Direct mix, partially offset by increased product costs and unfavorable foreign exchange impacts. APLA gross margin expanded 180 basis points from higher full-price ASP reflecting inflationary conditions, while Converse gross margin declined from higher product costs. Selling and administrative expenses decreased 7% as demand creation expense fell 18% due to reduced brand events and retail marketing, while operating overhead declined from lower wage-related costs and disciplined expense management.
material_events: Management completed organizational realignment under Consumer Direct Acceleration strategy, incurring restructuring charges with total expected costs of $315 million. The Brazil NIKE Brand business sale to Grupo SBF closed during the quarter, while Nike terminated the sale agreement for Argentina, Chile and Uruguay operations but maintains commitment to transition these markets to distributor partnerships. Nike temporarily suspended share repurchases to enhance liquidity and entered new $1 billion credit facility on March 15, 2021.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 continues creating business volatility through mandatory store closures affecting significant portions of EMEA owned stores and reduced physical retail traffic across regions. Global supply chain disruptions from container shortages, transportation delays, and port congestion interrupted inventory flow and delayed wholesale shipments. Foreign currency translation provided revenue and income benefits for the quarter from strengthening foreign currencies, while exchange rate fluctuations decreased gross margin by approximately 50 basis points.
forward_outlook: Management expects delayed wholesale shipments to occur in Q4 2021 and anticipates demand creation expense to increase in future periods as investment rebuilds toward pre-COVID levels. The company plans to reinvest annual wage-related savings from restructuring into strategic acceleration initiatives and expects to complete remaining organizational realignment actions by fiscal year-end 2021. Nike expects to resume share repurchases in fourth quarter fiscal 2021 with funding from operating cash flows.
uncertainty_profile: COVID-19 impacts remain uncertain with potential for material adverse effects on future revenue growth, profitability, and inventory levels across markets. The company faces ongoing supply chain volatility, potential revised wholesale customer payment terms, and higher sales-related reserves. Tax rate volatility continues from changing earnings mix across jurisdictions due to pandemic impacts, while future capital market volatility may increase commercial paper costs or affect debt market access.
